summary: Russian forces reported continued attacks on Ukrainian positions in Konstantinovka and destroyed Ukrainian UAV storage sites, while Ukrainian air defenses shot down 91 hostile drones across multiple fronts. Ukrainian units reported numerous Shahed and other UAV attacks targeting southern and eastern regions, with several UAVs intercepted and some striking locations such as Zaporizhzhia and Sumy. Both sides claim significant material losses, including Russian strikes on a chemical plant in Novomoskovsk and Ukrainian attacks on Russian fuel infrastructure near Ribinsk.
key_points: Russian forces continued destroying blocked Ukrainian formations in the southwest part of Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/380588).
Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 91 hostile UAVs of various types across the north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Ukrainian UAVs struck Russian fuel storage facilities near Ribinsk, approximately 270 km north of Moscow (Source: @eRadarrua/96485).
Ukrainian UAVs attacked the AZOT chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, Tula region, a key nitrate producer (Source: @eRadarrua/96487).
Russian air defenses downed 14 guided aviation bombs and 483 Ukrainian UAVs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving from the Black Sea towards Chornomorske and from the sea towards Odesa and Sumy regions (Source: @kpszsu/65151).
Russian attacks on Ukrainian UAV storage sites were reported (Source: @tass_agency/380585).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on Zaporizhzhia region and multiple incursions in Sumy and Kharkiv districts (Source: @kpszsu/65152, @kpszsu/65139).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces intensified operations, destroying Ukrainian formations in Konstantinovka and UAV storage sites, while claiming the downing of 14 guided bombs and 483 UAVs. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 91 hostile drones across the country, yet Ukrainian UAVs continued striking Russian targets, including a chemical plant in Novomoskovsk and fuel depots near Ribinsk. Both sides report substantial material losses, indicating a high‑intensity phase of the conflict.

points: Ukrainian air defense units shot down 91 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Iltamass types, across northern, southern, and eastern regions, with seven UAVs confirmed to have struck six locations (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Ukrainian UAVs launched attacks from the Black Sea towards Chornomorske and from the sea towards Odesa and Sumy, indicating maritime launch capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/65151).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV incursions in Sumy from the north and in the Shostkinsky district moving southwest, highlighting ongoing aerial pressure on northeastern fronts (Source: @kpszsu/65139).
Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, suggesting coordinated strike operations (Source: @kpszsu/65152).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted near the Liman area and the Red Liman settlement, targeting Russian infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAV attacks on the AZOT chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, a key nitrate production facility (Source: @eRadarrua/96487).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving from the sea towards Odesa and Sumy, indicating a broad operational reach across the southern and eastern axes (Source: @kpszsu/65151).
Russian forces continued destroying blocked Ukrainian formations in the southwest part of Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/380588).
Russian air defenses reported downing 14 guided aviation bombs and 483 Ukrainian UAVs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Russian forces claimed the destruction of Ukrainian UAV storage sites (Source: @tass_agency/380585).
Russian attacks targeted Ukrainian fuel and transport infrastructure, including a fuel depot in the Yaroslavl region (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Russian forces reported the elimination of two UAV command points in Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Russian air defense intercepted a UAV over Oryol that caused civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/380578).
Russian forces reported the destruction of Ukrainian UAV storage facilities in the Donetsk People's Republic using FAB‑3000 bombs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed approaching Sumy from the north and in the Shostkinsky district moving southwest, indicating pressure on the northeastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65139).
Ukrainian UAVs launched from the Black Sea towards the Chornomorske area in Kherson region, showing maritime‑to‑land strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/65153).
Russian forces continued attacks on Ukrainian positions in Konstantinovka, targeting blocked formations in the city's southwest (Source: @tass_agency/380588).
Ukrainian air defenses reported downing hostile UAVs over the eastern regions, including Shahed and other types (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Russian air defenses downed a UAV over Oryol, resulting in civilian casualties, reflecting the reach of UAV attacks into western Russian territory (Source: @tass_agency/380578).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted near the Liman area and Red Liman settlement, indicating ongoing strikes on Russian-held eastern localities (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on Zaporizhzhia region, a strategic eastern industrial area (Source: @kpszsu/65152).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 91 hostile UAVs across the north, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Iltamass types (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported in the Nikopol district of Dnipropetrovsk moving southward past Marhanets (Source: @kpszsu/65140).
Russian forces reported downing 249 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions during the night (Source: @tass_agency/380561).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed in the Chernihiv region, Korukivka district, heading south (Source: @kpszsu/65151).
Ukrainian UAVs were noted near the Liman area in the Red Liman settlement, indicating activity in the north‑west sector (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Russian air defenses intercepted a UAV over Oryol, causing civilian casualties, showing northern Russian exposure to UAV attacks (Source: @tass_agency/380578).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on Russian fuel storage near Ribinsk, located roughly 270 km north of Moscow (Source: @eRadarrua/96485).
Ukrainian UAVs launched from the Black Sea towards Chornomorske and South‑eastern Odesa, indicating maritime strike routes (Source: @kpszsu/65153).

Ukrainian UAVs struck the AZOT chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, Tula region, a key nitrate producer (Source: @eRadarrua/96487).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported near the Liman area and Red Liman settlement, targeting southern Russian positions (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Russian air defenses downed 14 guided aviation bombs and 483 UAVs, reflecting intense aerial contest in the south (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near the Black Sea heading towards the Odesa direction, indicating continued pressure on southern coastal areas (Source: @eRadarrua/96466).
Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 91 hostile UAVs of Shahed, Gerbera, Iltamass and other types across the country (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Russian air defenses claimed downing 14 guided aviation bombs and 483 Ukrainian UAVs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Russian forces reported destroying Ukrainian UAV storage sites and temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic using FAB‑3000 bombs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).

key_developments: Russian forces continued destroying blocked Ukrainian formations in Konstantinovka's southwest sector, indicating ongoing ground offensives (Source: @tass_agency/380588).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 91 hostile UAVs across multiple fronts, reflecting effective anti‑drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/65143).
Russian air defenses reported downing 14 guided bombs and 483 UAVs, showing intensified aerial contest (Source: @tass_agency/380590).
Ukrainian UAVs struck the AZOT chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, a key nitrate production facility (Source: @eRadarrua/96487).
Ukrainian UAVs attacked Russian fuel storage near Ribinsk, a strategic logistics hub north of Moscow (Source: @eRadarrua/96485).
Russian forces reported destroying Ukrainian UAV storage sites and dislocation points in Donetsk using FAB‑3000 bombs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Ukrainian UAVs launched from the Black Sea towards Odesa and Chornomorske, demonstrating maritime strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/65153).
sections.threat_assessment: Both sides are escalating UAV usage, with Ukraine employing maritime launch points to strike deep Russian targets, while Russia intensifies its air defense and counter‑UAV measures, claiming high UAV loss ratios. Russian ground operations continue in Konstantinovka, suggesting a focus on consolidating territorial gains. Ukrainian strikes on strategic Russian infrastructure, such as fuel depots and chemical plants, aim to disrupt logistics and production capabilities, raising the risk of broader industrial damage.
sections.regional_response: Local Russian authorities report civilian casualties from UAV attacks, exemplified by the Oryol incident, indicating heightened public concern. Ukrainian officials emphasize the need for reinforced air defenses amid increasing UAV threats, while also highlighting successful interceptions. International observers note the growing intensity of UAV warfare as a key factor shaping operational dynamics on both fronts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch corridors from the Black Sea remains critical, as multiple reports indicate maritime‑to‑land strike paths targeting southern Ukrainian and Russian positions. The reported destruction of Ukrainian UAV storage sites suggests Russian efforts to degrade Ukraine's drone operational capacity. Continued UAV activity over the northern and eastern fronts underscores the need for sustained air defense readiness across all sectors.
